nodes.name: DM_candidate_filter
nodes.description: Evaluates and filters dispersion measure (DM) candidates by comparing observed data against a base DM model, using sensitivity, channel frequency, bandwidth, and time sampling parameters to identify viable DM candidates.

inputs.name: data
inputs.type_desc: array-like — observed time-frequency or dedispersed data

inputs.name: data_base
inputs.type_desc: array-like — baseline reference data for comparison

inputs.name: sens
inputs.type_desc: float — sensitivity threshold for candidate detection

inputs.name: candidates
inputs.type_desc: array-like or list — input DM candidate values or indices to evaluate

inputs.name: fchan
inputs.type_desc: float or array-like — channel centre frequency or frequency array (MHz)

inputs.name: width
inputs.type_desc: float — pulse or channel width (MHz or samples)

outputs.name: dm_candidates_filtered
outputs.type_desc: array-like or list — filtered/validated DM candidates passing the sensitivity threshold

nodes.type_signature: (data: array-like — observed time-frequency or dedispersed data, data_base: array-like — baseline reference data for comparison, sens: float — sensitivity threshold for candidate detection, DM_base: float — base dispersion measure value, candidates: array-like or list — input DM candidate values or indices to evaluate, fchan: float or array-like — channel centre frequency or frequency array (MHz), width: float — pulse or channel width (MHz or samples), tsamp: float — sampling time (seconds)) -> array-like or list — filtered/validated DM candidates passing the sensitivity threshold
nodes.conceptual_summary: threshold_gated_reference_deviation_filter. Given an observed multi-dimensional signal array and a reference baseline array, this operation evaluates each element of an input candidate set by computing whether the observed signal's deviation from the baseline — modulated by a set of spectral-temporal resolution parameters (channel bandwidth, center frequency, and sampling interval) — exceeds a scalar sensitivity threshold. Candidates whose derived signal response falls below the threshold are rejected, yielding a pruned subset. The transform is a parametric, discriminative sieve: it maps a candidate index space through a physics-derived scoring function and returns only those candidates satisfying the inequality constraint.. Applications: Gravitational wave astronomy: filtering trial coalescence-time or chirp-mass candidates by comparing observed strain time-series against a waveform template baseline, gated by detector frequency band and sampling rate sensitivity — retaining only candidates whose SNR exceeds a detection threshold, Medical neuroimaging (EEG/MEG source localisation): evaluating a set of candidate dipole source locations by comparing modelled field maps against observed sensor arrays, using electrode bandwidth and temporal resolution to gate candidates below a signal-to-noise sensitivity bound, Radar / sonar signal processing: pruning a set of candidate target range-velocity hypotheses by comparing measured range-Doppler returns against a clutter baseline, with sensitivity scaled by pulse bandwidth and sampling interval — discarding sub-threshold false-alarm candidates, Seismology: screening candidate epicentre locations or fault-rupture depths by evaluating residual misfit between observed waveform envelopes and a reference Earth-model prediction, with threshold gating modulated by seismometer frequency response and sampling period
